intro: Today we are ranking Butter as a fat, per 100g, so you can see where it really lands. I know butter is technically a dairy, but I am classing it as oils and fats.

sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 81.1g. saturated fat and cholesterol stand out most. for a fat, the chemistry matters first but context still counts.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 81.1g. saturated fat and cholesterol stand out most. for a fat, the chemistry matters first but context still counts.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 0.1g. outside of that, the profile is pretty quiet. for oils and fats, chemistry is huge but context still matters.
sections.subtitleText: carbs is 0.1g. outside of that, the profile is pretty quiet. for oils and fats, chemistry is huge but context still matters.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 0.9g. everything else is fairly underwhelming. for a fat, the chemistry matters first but context still counts.
sections.subtitleText: protein is 0.9g. everything else is fairly underwhelming. for a fat, the chemistry matters first but context still counts.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin e at 11% DV, vitamin a at 28% DV stand out most. everything else here is pretty flat.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin e at 11% DV, vitamin a at 28% DV stand out most. everything else here is pretty flat.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: iron at 0% DV, magnesium at 0% DV stand out most. everything else here is pretty flat.
sections.subtitleText: iron at 0% DV, magnesium at 0% DV stand out most. everything else here is pretty flat.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: biggest pros here. simple traditional fat. very strong culinary usefulness. can fit as a moderate traditional fat.

displayItems.title: simple traditional fat
displayItems.explanation: This gives butter a cleaner identity than heavily processed fat substitutes.

displayItems.title: very strong culinary usefulness
displayItems.explanation: Butter has real kitchen legitimacy and practical use that matters in this category.

displayItems.title: can fit as a moderate traditional fat
displayItems.explanation: The editorial position here is that butter belongs as a flawed but legitimate middle-ground fat rather than a bottom-tier industrial product.

sections.subtitleText: biggest pros here. simple traditional fat. very strong culinary usefulness. can fit as a moderate traditional fat.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: biggest cons here. saturated fat is a major drawback. cholesterol is high. fat profile is clearly weaker than top-tier oils.

displayItems.title: saturated fat is a major drawback
displayItems.explanation: This is the main reason butter cannot be an elite oils-and-fats food.

displayItems.title: cholesterol is high
displayItems.explanation: A second meaningful strike against the fat profile.

displayItems.title: fat profile is clearly weaker than top-tier oils
displayItems.explanation: Butter is not competing with the best oils on chemistry alone.

sections.subtitleText: biggest cons here. saturated fat is a major drawback. cholesterol is high. fat profile is clearly weaker than top-tier oils.

closing.summary: So overall, Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
closing.finalReveal: That is a B tier food.
closing.useCaseNote: This food lands in B tier with an overall score of 27 from a base nutrition score of 15. Context lifted it by 12 points.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
